journal.name: Journal
journal.desc:
This is Joe's Journal.

Joe keeps track of everything here.

press_card.name: Press Card
press_card.desc:
Joe Constantino,
Reporter for The Times.

Valid until: August 1971

Without this, the guards at Congress
won't let him in.

wallet.name: Wallet
wallet.desc:
A typical Filipino wallet.

Contains a few hundred pesos, identific
-ation cards, and pictures of family.

flashlight.name: Flashlight
flashlight.desc: Can be used to shine light on dark areas.

note.name: Ric's note
note.desc:
President Ferdinand Marcos' State of
the Nation Address will happen on the
Congress Building, located along:

Padre Burgos Drive
Ermita
1000 Manila

estimate_count.name: Number of demonstrators
estimate_count.desc:
If you’d ask me, I’d say there’s around
20,000 of them.

no_guns.name: No guns
no_guns.desc:
Being with the demonstrators did not
make me feel uneasy.

None of them carried guns.

podium_nusp.name: NUSP President
podium_nusp.desc:
He’s Edgar Jopson, the head of the
National Union of Students of
the Philippines (NUSP).

effigies_general.name: Creative effigies
effigies_general.desc:
The student activists are at it again with
their effigies.

What could the coffin represent?

demo_objective.name: Rally demands
demo_objective.desc:
The NUSP is here to appeal for a nonpartisan
Constitutional Convention from the president
and to ask him to refrain from running for a third term.

effigies_meaning.name: Effigy symbolism
effigies_meaning.desc:
The cardboard coffin represents the death of democracy.
The cardboard crocodile symbolizes the cronies and
greed of congressmen for money.

invited_orgs.name: Allied youth groups
invited_orgs.desc:
Other organizations present include KM
(Kabataan Makabayan) and the SDK
(Samahang Demokratiko ng Kabataan).

cops_reputation.name: Police street rep
cops_reputation.desc:
Two words – corrupt and brutal.
They serve only the interests of the rich and powerful.

e1.title: SONA Location
e1.description:
IT WAS FIVE MINUTES PAST FIVE in the afternoon,
by the clock on the Maharnilad tower, when I
arrived at [?].

e1.title: Session Hall Doors
e1.description: The doors of the session hall were [?].

e1.title: Crowd Estimate
e1.description:
There were, according to conservative estimates,
 at least [?] of them, perhaps even 50,000.

e1.title: Speaker
e1.description:
There were two mikes, taped together; … They were
in the hands of [?] Jopson of the National Union of Students of the Philippines,
the group that had organized the rally and secured the permit for it.

e1.title: Demonstrators' guns
e1.description:
Among the demonstrators it was possible to feel at
ease. [?] of them carried guns, they didn’t stand on ceremony,
 and there was no need for the aura of privilege that a press badge automatically
confers on its wearer.

e1.title: Chief Objective
e1.description: Its chief objective was to demand [?].

e1.title: Effigies
e1.description:
WHERE THE DEMONSTRATION LEADERS STOOD, emblems of the
enemy were prominently displayed: a cardboard [?] representing the
death of democracy at the hands of the goonstabulary in the last elections.

e1.title: Weapons in the Chaos
e1.description:
The demonstrators who got as far as the sidewalk that led
to the Muni golf links started to pick up pebbles and rocks with
which they pelted the police. Very soon, placards had
turned into missiles, and the sound of broken glass punctuated the yelling [?] were flying, too.
The effigy was down on the ground, still burning.

e1.title: Perception of cops
e1.description: … the cops are generally believed to be [?]

e1.title: Demonstartors' vehicle
e1.description:
The demonstrators had hired a [?] in which rode some of
their leaders. It had two loudspeakers on its roof,
was surrounded by students, and inched its way
forward and backward throughout the melee.

e1.title: Cops actions
e1.description:
When the cops were through [?] the jeepney’s passengers,
they backed away.

e1.title: Injured People Count
e1.description:
More than [?] demonstrators with bloody heads
and broken wrists had been or were being treated along with
three or four policemen hit by rocks.

joe_intro2.text: I need to make sure that I didn’t forget anything before I go.

joe_target_board.text:
The bulletin board. Everyone's kept up to date with everything
that's happening, thanks to that.

joe_newspapers.text: They keep copies of today's edition in the rack.

joe_joe.text: I don't think anything useful will come out of clicking me.

joe_light1.text: There's nothing wrong with the light fixtures, as far as I'm concerned.

joe_light2.text: They are your standard, run-of-the-mill lights.

joe_tut2.text:
You might want to CLICK on the desk and take everything that's
important: my press card, journal, pen, and the like.

joe_desk.text: Let's see... I'll take my press card, journal, pen, and wallet.

joe_item_stapler.text: I don't really need to take a stapler with me.

joe_item_wallet.text: I inherited this from my father. It surely stood the test of time.

joe_item_reports.text: I need to bring this to the archives. On second thought, maybe later.

joe_item_typewriter.text: An Olympia typewriter. This is where I write all my draft reports.

joe_item_mail.text: I don't have anything new at the moment.

joe_item_pencils.text: Of course, I couldn't write without a pen.

joe_item_pencils_empty.text: All that's left is a couple of pencils.

joe_item_papers.text: A pile of empty papers waiting for something to be written.

joe_item_journal.text: Got it. I can't leave without my journal.

joe_item_press_card.text: Yes, that’s me. Joe Constantino, reporting for The Times.

joe_item_note.text: The note says the place of my assignment.

joe_item_flashlight.text: This flashlight will certainly be useful in the future.

joe_return.text: On second thought, I might have forgotten something.

tut_hovercursor.text:
Hover over the items in your screen to perform an action on them.
The cursor changes based on its location.

tut_rightclickreturn.text:
You can return to the previous area by clicking on the empty spots in the screen,
right clicking anywhere, or on areas with a zoom-out icon as the cursor.

joe_choice1.text: Manong, take me to Luneta Park.

joe_choice2.text: Manong, take me to the Congress building.

driver_choice2.text:
I can only give you a ride until Luneta. The entire stretch of the road is
occupied by demonstrators.

joe_choice3_2.text: On second thought, I don’t need to go home right now.

driver_choice3_5.text: Where are you headed to, then?

driver_pay1.text: We’re here, sir. Pay up, please.

joe_pay1.text: Let me just get my wallet.

joe_pay2b.text: It looks like I forgot my wallet.

joe_text2.text: It’s already five minutes past five.

joe_target_speaker.text: The president’s already delivering his SONA message.

joe_target_estimate_count.text: If you’d ask me, I’d say there’s around 20,000 of them.

joe_target_no_guns.text: None of the demonstrators carried guns.

joe_target_effigies.text: The student activists are at it again with their effigies.

joe_target_person_speaking1.text:
He’s Edgar Jopson, the head of the National Union of Students
of the Philippines (NUSP).

joe_target_person_speaking2.text: They’re the group that organized and secured a permit for the rally.

joe_target_police.text:
They’re uniformed policemen, swinging their long rattan sticks, along
with members of the riot squad.

joe_intro.text: I'm Joe Constantino, from The Times. You need my press card?

police_mac_wp_1.text: Unfortunately, Mr. Constantino, the session hall is already closed.

police_mac_wp_2.text:
Letting you in means letting everyone in, including those photographers who
arrived late. We cannot let you and everyone else disturb the order inside.

joe_np_1.text:
Uh. It’s not in my pockets. I seem to have forgotten my press card at my desk back
in the office.

police_mac_np_1.text: You must be one of the militants pretending to be with the press.

police_mac_np_2.text: Please leave now if you don’t want any trouble.

joe_intro.text: Maybe I should speak with one of the demonstrators instead.

joe_talk1.text: I am Joe from The Times. I want to get your side of this protest.

joe_talk2.text: Let us keep this straight and on the record. What is your story?

dan_talk1.text: Yeah, all right. Go ahead and ask what you need to know.

dan_talk2.text: Ask it straight, Sir Joe, and I will give you straight answers.

joe_outro_result.text: That wraps my questions. I have enough here to write a clean piece.

dan_outro_result.text: We are done? All right. Just make sure the paper prints the truth.

joe_q1_text1.text: You are a member of what organization out here?

joe_q1_text2.text: I need the exact name so I can put it down right in my notes.

dan_q1_text2.text: The National Union of Students of the Philippines.

joe_q1_doubt_talk1.text: Are you sure about that? A lot of groups are claiming this crowd.

dan_q1_doubt_talk1.text: Of course I am sure. The NUSP is the reason why I am standing here.

joe_q1_fact_talk1.text: NUSP, understood. Your group secured the permit for this rally.

dan_q1_fact_talk1.text: Yes, we did. We are trying to reach as many people as possible.

joe_q1_accuse_talk1.text:
Let us drop the act. You are an undercover communist cadre.
You are just here to stir up trouble and destabilize things.

dan_q1_accuse_talk1.text: How exactly are you going to prove a wild claim like that?

dan_q1_accuse_talk2.text: That is absurd. You cannot just brand us all as subversives.

joe_q2_talk1.text: What is the actual objective of this demonstration?

joe_q2_talk2.text: What exactly are you demanding from the President today?

dan_q2_talk1.text:
We are here to appeal for a nonpartisan Constitutional Convention.
We are also asking him to refrain from running for a third term.

dan_q2_talk2.text: That is our demand, and we have been shouting it all afternoon.

joe_q2_doubt_talk1.text:
That is the public line. Is there something more to your cause?
Are you planning on overthrowing the government?

dan_q2_doubt_talk1.text: Not true at all. You have a very wide imagination, Sir Joe.

joe_q2_fact_talk1.text: A nonpartisan convention and term limits. That is clear enough.

dan_q2_fact_talk1.text: Yes. We will not settle for anything less than what is right.

joe_q2_accuse_talk1.text:
Your real objective is to manipulate the minds of the people.
The convention is just a front for your real plans.

dan_q2_accuse_talk1.text: I do not get it. Our demands are printed on every banner here.

joe_q3_talk1.text: For you, what do those effigies actually symbolize?

joe_q3_talk2.text: Start with the coffin. People can read that in many ways.

dan_q3_talk1.text: The cardboard coffin represents the death of our democracy.

dan_q3_talk2.text: The crocodile symbolizes his cronies and greedy congressmen.

dan_q3_talk3.text: They are warnings about the state of our nation.

joe_q3_doubt_talk1.text: The coffin... you are not planning on putting the President in it?

dan_q3_doubt_talk1.text: Where are these questions coming from? It is just cardboard.

joe_q3_accuse_talk1.text:
That coffin imagery is a direct threat to the President's life.
You are inciting this crowd to commit violence.

dan_q3_accuse_talk1.text: It stands for a dead democracy, not a person. You are twisting it.

joe_q4_talk1.text: What about the other organizations out here today?

joe_q4_talk2.text: Who else is coordinating with your group on the ground?

dan_q4_talk1.text: I am not familiar with everyone, but there is KM and the SDK.

dan_q4_talk2.text: It is a big crowd. I only know some of them by name.

joe_q4_doubt_talk1.text: I do not think so. You know them because you are with them.

dan_q4_doubt_talk1.text: That makes no sense. Sharing a street does not mean I know everyone.

joe_q4_accuse_talk1.text: You are deliberately withholding the names of the other organizers.

dan_q4_accuse_talk1.text: Attending the same rally does not make me their spokesman, Sir Joe.

joe_q4_fact_talk1.text: Youth groups and student unions. It is notable you work as one.

dan_q4_fact_talk1.text: Unity is our only strength against this administration.

joe_q3_fact_talk1.text: A coffin for democracy and a crocodile for Congress. Very clear.

dan_q3_fact_talk1.text: Exactly. We want the public to see the greed destroying the country.

joe_q5_fact_talk1.text: You see the police line as a clear threat to the students, then.

dan_q5_fact_talk1.text: Just watch them grip their rattan sticks and tell me I am wrong.

joe_q5_talk1.text: What do you think of the cops here to maintain the peace?

joe_q5_talk2.text: From where you stand, how are they handling this crowd?

dan_q5_talk1.text: Corrupt and brutal. They only serve the rich and powerful.

dan_q5_talk2.text: They are not here to protect us. They are here to pressure us.

joe_q5_doubt_talk1.text:
I think it is the other way around. You are framing them.
You are making the police look bad to gain sympathy.

dan_q5_doubt_talk1.text: Sir Joe, if you are here to defend the police, please just go away.

joe_q5_accuse_talk1.text: You are provoking the riot squad so you can cry police brutality.

dan_q5_accuse_talk1.text: We are fighting peacefully, and you are throwing wild accusations.

joe_q6_talk1.text: How many people are here in your estimate?

joe_q6_talk2.text: It is difficult to get a good count from this distance.

dan_q6_talk1.text: I would say there are at least 50,000 students and workers here.

dan_q6_talk2.text: The entire stretch of the road is occupied by the people.

joe_q6_doubt_talk1.text:
Fifty thousand? That seems very high for this area.
I have seen smaller crowds look much larger than this.

dan_q6_doubt_talk1.text: Count them yourself. The crowd runs all the way down to Luneta.

joe_q6_accuse_clue1.text: You are inflating those numbers just to make your group look bigger.

dan_q6_accuse_clue1.text: Look at the streets! How can you possibly say we are a minority?

joe_q6_accuse_success1.text: My own estimates place the crowd much closer to 20,000 people.

dan_q6_accuse_success1.text:
Maybe my enthusiasm got the better of me.
But 20,000 is still a massive force that they need to reckon with.

joe_q6_fact_talk1.text: It is a massive turnout today, whichever estimate we use.

dan_q6_fact_talk1.text: We are out here to be heard by the President, not to cause trouble.

joe_q7_talk1.text: Who is actually leading this effort today?

joe_q7_talk2.text: Someone secured the permit and planned this. Give me a name.

dan_q7_talk1.text: Just students who care about the country. We do not have one boss.

dan_q7_talk2.text: Different groups handle different tasks on the ground.

joe_q7_doubt_talk1.text: A rally this coordinated usually has identifiable organizers.

dan_q7_doubt_talk1.text: The will of the people is the brain behind this, Sir Joe.

joe_q7_accuse_clue1.text: You are shielding your coordinators from scrutiny by the authorities.

joe_q7_accuse_success1.text: I recognize Edgar Jopson at the PA system. He is the NUSP head.

dan_q7_accuse_success1.text: Yes, Edgar is our president. But he is just one of many voices here.

dan_q7_accuse_clue1.text: We are not hiding anyone. Our leaders stand at the podium up front.

joe_q7_fact_talk1.text: So the leadership is distributed, not centered on one person alone.

dan_q7_fact_talk1.text: We are well organized, Sir Joe. You do not have to worry about us.

joe_q8_talk1.text: I saw a jeepney with loudspeakers moving with your crowd.

joe_q8_talk2.text: It looked like a command vehicle. Who was inside?

dan_q8_talk1.text: A jeepney? I have not seen one. We walked all the way here.

dan_q8_talk2.text: If there was a vehicle, it was not ours.

joe_q8_doubt_talk1.text: That jeepney was heavily guarded by your members.

dan_q8_doubt_talk1.text: Streets are packed. People protect what stands out.

joe_q8_accuse_clue1.text: That jeepney is directing the mob's movements.

joe_accuse_backdown.text: I am dropping this. Let us talk about something else.

dan_q8_accuse_clue1.text: It is just a jeepney with a microphone on top.

joe_q8_fact_talk1.text: A mobile sound system makes sense for a crowd this loud.

dan_q8_fact_talk1.text: Exactly. We have enough to worry about just marching.

joe_q9_talk1.text: So this is an entirely peaceful demonstration? No weapons at all?

joe_q9_talk2.text: Not even improvised ones hidden away in those bags?

dan_q9_talk1.text: None at all. We are here to protest our grievances, not to fight.

dan_q9_talk2.text: People brought placards, food, and some water. That is it.

joe_q9_fact_talk1.text: Peaceful intent noted. I truly hope all sides hold to that promise.

dan_q9_fact_talk1.text: We can only hope for the best, Sir Joe.

joe_q9_doubt_talk1.text: Are you sure no one is carrying anything? This crowd is very tense.

dan_q9_doubt_talk1.text: I assure you. We left our weapons at home because we have none.

joe_q9_accuse_talk1.text: Witnesses insist there are concealed weapons within your ranks.

dan_q9_accuse_talk1.text: That is a very serious accusation. Search us all if you must.

joe_q1_fact_talk2.text: All right, I have the NUSP properly noted in my draft.

joe_q1_doubt_talk2.text: I am looking for a precise answer, not a rehearsed slogan.

joe_q1_accuse_talk2.text: If you are not subversives, give me details I can actually verify.

joe_q2_fact_talk2.text: That is a concrete demand. I can report that clearly in the paper.

joe_q2_doubt_talk2.text: You need to convince me there is no second track to this protest.

joe_q2_accuse_talk2.text: If I am wrong about your motives, let your actions prove it.

joe_q3_fact_talk2.text: It is a symbolic protest, then. Not a literal or direct threat.

joe_q3_doubt_talk2.text: In a tense and crowded street, symbols can be misread very quickly.

joe_q3_accuse_talk2.text: If you keep parading that coffin, people will read a threat into it.

joe_q4_fact_talk2.text: A broad coalition of youth, even with your internal differences.

joe_q4_doubt_talk2.text: You are a leader here. You likely know far more names than that.

joe_q4_accuse_talk2.text: Right now, your silence just reads like protection of your network.

joe_q5_fact_talk2.text: I will make sure to record that as your official account.

joe_q5_doubt_talk2.text: I need specific incidents of brutality, not just your impressions.

joe_q5_accuse_talk2.text: That language hardens both sides before the facts are even checked.

joe_q6_fact_talk2.text: It is certainly a crowd large enough to matter politically.

joe_q6_doubt_talk2.text: I cannot print massive numbers in the paper that I cannot defend.

joe_q6_accuse_clue2.text: Those numbers shift too easily for me to trust your overall account.

joe_q7_fact_talk2.text: Even a loosely led group can still coordinate a massive movement.

joe_q7_doubt_talk2.text: Movements that are this tightly coordinated rarely run leaderless.

joe_q7_accuse_clue2.text: Give me the names of your coordinators, and we can test the claim.

joe_q8_fact_talk2.text: That explains how your messages traveled so fast through the line.

joe_q8_doubt_talk2.text: Then who was operating that vehicle right in the middle of the crowd?

joe_q8_accuse_clue2.text: Treating that loudspeaker jeep as purely incidental is not credible.

joe_q9_fact_talk2.text: That is good. I would much rather report on restraint than on blood.

joe_q9_doubt_talk2.text: In chaos like this, one hidden weapon changes the entire narrative.

joe_q9_accuse_talk2.text: If any weapons surface today, your claim of a peaceful rally fails.

dan_q1_fact_talk2.text: Just write that we are concerned students, not paid infiltrators.

dan_q1_doubt_talk2.text: You can ask anyone else on this street and get the exact same answer.

dan_q2_fact_talk2.text: A Con-Con in 1971, and no third term for Marcos. Keep it straight.

dan_q2_doubt_talk2.text: The banners already say exactly that. We are not hiding our goals.

dan_q2_accuse_talk2.text: Read the placards first before you accuse us of having hidden plans.

dan_q3_fact_talk2.text: The symbols criticize the people in power, not anyone in this crowd.

dan_q3_doubt_talk2.text: If we actually wanted violence, we would not be using cardboard.

dan_q3_accuse_talk2.text: You are deliberately twisting our protest symbols into a violent act.

dan_q4_fact_talk2.text: We are different groups, but we share the exact same demands today.

dan_q4_doubt_talk2.text: This is a massive rally ground, Sir Joe. Not a strict roll call.

dan_q4_accuse_talk2.text: Knowing a few names does not make me a handler for the entire crowd.

dan_q5_fact_talk2.text: Just watch how they grip their batons and tell me I am wrong again.

dan_q5_doubt_talk2.text: Professional restraint is not what we see from this police line.

dan_q5_accuse_talk2.text: Calling out police abuse by its real name is not a crime, Sir Joe.

dan_q6_fact_talk2.text: Exact numbers aside, this is still a massive turnout for our cause.

dan_q6_doubt_talk2.text: The avenue is full of people from one end all the way to the other.

dan_q6_accuse_clue2.text: You are quibbling over exact figures just to dodge the real message.

dan_q6_accuse_success2.text: Fine, print 20,000 then. Our demand for a convention still stands.

dan_q7_fact_talk2.text: Our coordination is a collective effort, not some secret command.

dan_q7_doubt_talk2.text: Our organization is entirely visible if you actually look around us.

dan_q7_accuse_clue2.text: You want a hidden chief to fit the dirty story you came with.

dan_q7_accuse_success2.text: Yes, Edgar is a prominent voice, but he surely does not speak alone.

dan_q8_fact_talk2.text: A good sound system helps the people hear the speeches, not attack.

dan_q8_doubt_talk2.text: Crowds merge and split all day. Not every single jeep belongs to us.

dan_q8_accuse_clue2.text: A loudspeaker jeep does not make a grand conspiracy.

dan_q9_fact_talk2.text: Our whole point today is to be seen and heard, not to come out armed.

dan_q9_doubt_talk2.text: Search us thoroughly, and you will only find leaflets and raincoats.

dan_q9_accuse_talk2.text: Do not turn your own paranoia and fear into false evidence, Sir Joe.

joe_intro.text: It’s probably seven o’clock. It’s difficult to see anything.

joe_target_jeep.text:
It’s likely that the jeep was rented out by the demonstrators.
It had two loudspeakers on the roof.

joe_target_riot_talk2.text:
The cops, wearing crash helmets and carrying wicker shields, were
striking out and beating up the students surrounding the jeepney.

joe_target_demonstrator_talk1.text:
He’s downed and crumpled to the ground, his legs to his chest and his
arms over his head.

joe_target_demonstrator_talk2.text: Is that blood? He must’ve been beaten up by those cops.

cop2_end.text: Come closer and you’ll get what you’re looking for.

doc_intro1.text: PGH, Mr. Constantino. You were brought here after passing out.

joe_target_demonstrator.text:
He's one of them. I’d estimate there’s around thirty or so demonstrators
with bloody heads and broken wrists.

joe_target_nusp_talk1.text:
I thought they’ve left early. It seems like some members of the NUSP
weren’t able to escape the violence.

joe_target_police_talk1.text: I see maybe three to four policemen, possibly hit by rocks.

joe_target_doctor_talk1.text: She’s probably busy speaking with the other patients.

joe_target_door_talk1.text: I’ll be on my way, thank you.
